description: Here we are integrating the CSOD domain into the Data Modelling framework to enable seamless data engineering, semantic enrichment, and business intelligence capabilities.

tables.name: Document Slack Conversation
tables.description: This table stores data related to conversations held on Slack regarding various documents, capturing essential details such as document identifiers, content, participants, and key discussion points.

columns.name: id
columns.description: Unique identifier for each Slack conversation document entry.

business_rules: Can be null, as not all conversations may have an ID assigned.
Should be unique within the context of the document table.
data_quality_checks: Check for null values where applicable.
Ensure that IDs are integers and follow the expected format.
metadata.business_description: This column represents the ID assigned to each conversation document, which helps in tracking and referencing specific conversations within the learning and talent management context.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by document_id to retrieve specific conversation details
Filter by date_range to analyze conversations over time
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of conversations by document_id
GROUP BY participants to analyze engagement

columns.name: document_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for each document in the Document Slack Conversation table, represented as a UUID.

business_rules: Can be null, as not all documents may have an ID assigned.
Should be unique if provided, to ensure accurate document identification.
data_quality_checks: Check for null values where applicable.
Validate UUID format for consistency.
metadata.business_description: This column represents the unique ID assigned to each document, which is essential for tracking and referencing specific documents within the context of training and talent management discussions.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by document name
Filter by date range

columns.name: created_at
columns.description: Timestamp indicating when the Slack conversation document was created.

business_rules: Can be null if the document was created without a timestamp.
Should be populated for all documents that have a creation date.
data_quality_checks: Ensure the timestamp is in the correct format (ISO 8601).
Check for any null values in documents that should have a creation date.
metadata.business_description: This column represents the date and time when the document of the Slack conversation was generated, providing context for the timing of discussions and decisions made within the conversation.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by specific date ranges to analyze conversations within a timeframe
Filter by participants to see when documents were created by specific users
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of documents created over a time period
GROUP BY date to analyze document creation trends

columns.name: document_name
columns.description: Field representing the name of the document associated with Slack conversations.

business_rules: Can be null if no document is associated with the conversation
Should be descriptive enough to identify the document's content
data_quality_checks: Ensure the document name is not excessively long (e.g., limit to 255 characters)
Check for leading or trailing whitespace
Validate that the document name does not contain special characters that may cause issues in processing
metadata.business_description: This column captures the title or name of the document that is being discussed or referenced in Slack conversations related to training and talent management.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by specific document names
Filter by date range of conversations

columns.name: document_content
columns.description: Field containing the text content of the document related to Slack conversations.

business_rules: Can be null if no content is available
Should contain relevant information pertaining to training and talent management
data_quality_checks: Ensure content is relevant and related to the training topics
Check for excessive length or irrelevant information
metadata.business_description: This column holds the actual content of documents generated from Slack conversations, which may include discussions, decisions, and insights relevant to training and talent management.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by document_id
Filter by date_range

columns.name: channel
columns.description: Text field representing the communication channel used in the Slack conversation.

business_rules: Can be null if the channel is not specified
Should contain valid channel names as per Slack's naming conventions
data_quality_checks: Check for empty values where applicable
Validate against known Slack channel names to ensure accuracy
metadata.business_description: This column indicates the specific channel within Slack where the conversation took place, which can help in understanding the context and audience of the discussion.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by specific channels to focus on targeted discussions
Filter by date range to analyze conversations over time
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT by channel to analyze conversation frequency
GROUP BY channel to assess engagement levels

columns.name: date_range
columns.description: A text field representing a range of dates relevant to the document's context.

business_rules: Can be null if the date range is not applicable or unknown
Should follow a consistent format (e.g., 'YYYY-MM-DD to YYYY-MM-DD') if provided
data_quality_checks: Validate format if a date range is provided
Check for overlapping date ranges in related documents
metadata.business_description: This column captures the time frame during which the Slack conversation took place, providing context for the document's content and relevance to training and talent management activities.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by specific date ranges to analyze conversations within a certain timeframe
Filter by document relevance to specific training programs or compliance requirements

columns.name: participants
columns.description: An array of individuals who participated in the Slack conversation related to the document.

business_rules: Can contain multiple entries or be null if no participants are involved.
Each entry should represent a valid participant identifier (e.g., user ID or email).
data_quality_checks: Ensure that the array does not contain duplicate participant identifiers.
Validate that each participant identifier conforms to the expected format (e.g., email or user ID).
metadata.business_description: This column captures the names or identifiers of participants involved in discussions about the document, providing context for collaboration and input on the content.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by specific participants
Filter by documents with no participants
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of unique participants per document
LIST of participants involved in each document

columns.name: key_topics
columns.description: An array of key topics discussed in the Slack conversation related to training and talent management.

business_rules: Can contain multiple topics or be null
Each topic should be relevant to training and talent management
data_quality_checks: Ensure topics are relevant and meaningful
Check for excessive null values in the dataset
metadata.business_description: This column captures the main subjects or themes that are relevant to the training and talent management discussions within the Slack conversations. It helps in identifying the focus areas of conversations and can be used to analyze trends in training topics.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by specific topics
Filter by date range of conversations
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of unique topics
GROUP BY document_id to analyze topic distribution

columns.name: summary
columns.description: A text field that provides a brief overview or summary of the Slack conversation related to the document.

business_rules: Can be null if no summary is provided
Should be concise and relevant to the document content
data_quality_checks: Ensure the summary is relevant to the document
Check for excessive length or irrelevant content
metadata.business_description: This column captures the essence of discussions held in Slack regarding the document, allowing users to quickly understand the main points without reading the entire conversation.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by document_id to find summaries related to specific documents
Filter by date_range to analyze summaries from specific time periods

columns.name: action_items
columns.description: An array of action items discussed during the Slack conversation related to training and talent management.

business_rules: Can contain multiple action items or be null
Each action item should be a clear and actionable task
data_quality_checks: Ensure that action items are relevant to the conversation context
Validate that action items are not excessively vague or ambiguous
metadata.business_description: This column captures specific tasks or follow-up actions that need to be taken as a result of discussions in the Slack conversation. It is essential for tracking commitments and ensuring accountability in the learning and talent management processes.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by document_id to see action items related to specific documents
Filter by date_range to analyze action items over time
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of action items per conversation
LIST of unique action items across conversations

columns.name: id
columns.description: id field

columns.name: document_id
columns.description: document id field

columns.name: created_at
columns.description: created at field

columns.name: document_name
columns.description: document name field

columns.name: document_content
columns.description: document content field

columns.name: channel
columns.description: channel field

columns.name: date_range
columns.description: date range field

columns.name: participants
columns.description: participants field

columns.name: key_topics
columns.description: key topics field

columns.name: summary
columns.description: summary field

columns.name: action_items
columns.description: action items field

metadata.data_lineage: Data is sourced from Slack conversations related to documents, with integration processes that extract, transform, and load (ETL) this information into the table for analysis and reporting.
metadata.data_retention: Data is retained for a period of 5 years to comply with training and compliance regulations.
access_patterns: Frequent queries by Learning & Development teams for insights on document discussions.
Periodic reporting by HR and Compliance Officers to ensure adherence to training standards.
metadata.business_purpose: The purpose of this table is to centralize communication data related to document discussions, enabling Learning & Development teams to track conversations, extract insights, and ensure that all relevant information is captured for training and compliance purposes.

key_relationships: Links to training records and transcripts to provide context on document usage.
Associates with employee skills and competency tracking to align discussions with training needs.
primary_use_cases: Tracking discussions around training documents to ensure all feedback is captured.
Analyzing key topics and action items from Slack conversations to improve training materials.
Generating summaries of document discussions for reporting and compliance purposes.

semantic_description.description: This table captures data related to conversations held in Slack about specific documents, enabling organizations to track discussions, decisions, and action items associated with document collaboration.

key_columns.name: id
key_columns.description: Unique identifier for each conversation entry
key_columns.business_significance: Acts as the primary key to ensure each record is distinct and can be referenced accurately

key_columns.name: document_id
key_columns.description: Identifier linking the conversation to a specific document
key_columns.business_significance: Enables tracking of discussions related to individual documents, essential for document management and review processes

key_columns.name: created_at
key_columns.description: Timestamp indicating when the conversation entry was created
key_columns.business_significance: Provides context for the timing of discussions, which is crucial for understanding the document review timeline

key_columns.name: document_name
key_columns.description: Name of the document associated with the conversation
key_columns.business_significance: Helps in identifying the document being discussed, enhancing clarity in communication records

key_columns.name: summary
key_columns.description: A brief overview of the conversation's content
key_columns.business_significance: Facilitates quick understanding of the discussion without needing to read the entire conversation, aiding in efficient information retrieval

key_columns.name: action_items
key_columns.description: List of actionable tasks derived from the conversation
key_columns.business_significance: Critical for tracking follow-up tasks and ensuring accountability in document-related discussions

semantic_description.table_purpose: To serve as a repository for Slack conversation data linked to documents, facilitating the management of communication and collaboration around document creation and review.
semantic_description.business_context: This table is part of a document management and collaboration system, where Slack is used as a communication tool. It allows teams to document conversations about specific documents, ensuring that all relevant discussions, decisions, and tasks are recorded and easily accessible for future reference. This enhances collaboration, accountability, and project management efficiency.

suggested_relationships.reasoning: Each document can have multiple associated conversations, allowing for comprehensive tracking of discussions and decisions related to that document.
Participants in conversations can be multiple users, and a user can participate in discussions about various documents, facilitating a network of collaboration.

performance_considerations: Ensure efficient indexing on key columns like document_id and created_at for faster query performance.
Monitor the size of the participants and action_items arrays to optimize storage and retrieval times.

recommendations: At least 2 tables are required for relationship analysis

tables.name: Training Data
tables.description: This table stores comprehensive training-related data for employees, including details about training assignments, completion statuses, and managerial oversight. It serves as a central repository for tracking employee training and development activities.

columns.name: id
columns.description: Unique identifier for each training record in the Training Data table.

business_rules: Can be null, as not all training records may have an ID assigned.
Should be unique within the context of the training records.
data_quality_checks: Check for null values where applicable.
Ensure that IDs are unique across the training records.
metadata.business_description: This column represents the ID assigned to each training record, which helps in tracking and managing training sessions for employees.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by training type
Filter by assigned date
Filter by completion status
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of training records by training type
COUNT of training records by division

columns.name: full_name
columns.description: A field that contains the complete name of the individual, typically formatted as 'First Last'.

business_rules: Can be null if the individual prefers not to disclose their name
Should not contain special characters or numbers
data_quality_checks: Check for leading or trailing spaces
Validate that the name does not exceed 100 characters
Ensure that the name is not empty if provided
metadata.business_description: This column represents the full name of the employee or participant in the training program, which is essential for identifying individuals in training records.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by training type
Filter by division

columns.name: user_id
columns.description: A unique identifier for users in the training data system.

business_rules: Can be null, as not all users may have an associated training record.
Should be unique within the context of the training data table.
data_quality_checks: Check for null values to ensure proper linkage to user records.
Validate format to ensure it adheres to expected user ID patterns.
metadata.business_description: This column represents the user identification number associated with each individual participating in training programs. It is essential for tracking user-specific training records and linking them to other relevant data points.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by user ID to analyze training records for specific individuals.
Filter by training type to assess user participation in different training programs.
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of unique user IDs to determine the number of participants in training programs.

columns.name: manager_name
columns.description: The name of the manager responsible for overseeing the training of employees.

business_rules: Can be null if no manager is assigned
Should reflect the current manager's name for accurate reporting
data_quality_checks: Ensure the name is formatted correctly (e.g., no special characters)
Validate against a list of active managers to avoid outdated entries
metadata.business_description: This column captures the name of the manager associated with the training records, providing context for managerial oversight and accountability in employee training and development.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by specific manager to analyze training effectiveness
Filter by training type to see manager involvement
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of training sessions by manager
GROUP BY manager_name for reporting purposes

columns.name: training_type
columns.description: Field indicating the type of training provided to employees, such as online, in-person, or hybrid.

business_rules: Can be null if the training type is not specified
Should align with predefined training categories established by the Learning & Talent Management team
data_quality_checks: Ensure values are consistent with the defined training types
Check for typos or variations in training type descriptions
metadata.business_description: This column categorizes the training sessions attended by employees, helping to identify the format and delivery method of the training.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by specific training types to analyze trends
Filter by completion status to assess effectiveness of training types
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT by training type
GROUP BY training type to analyze participation rates

columns.name: curriculum_title
columns.description: Text field representing the title of the curriculum associated with training programs.

business_rules: Can be null, but should be populated when applicable
Should accurately reflect the curriculum being referenced
data_quality_checks: Ensure that the title is descriptive and relevant to the training content
Check for consistency in naming conventions across different curricula
metadata.business_description: The curriculum title is used to identify and categorize training programs within the Learning & Talent Management domain. It helps in organizing training records and provides clarity on the content being delivered to employees.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by curriculum title to view specific training programs
Filter by training type to analyze curricula related to specific training categories
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of training programs by curriculum title
GROUP BY curriculum title to analyze training distribution

columns.name: training_title
columns.description: Field representing the title of the training program or course attended by an employee.

business_rules: Can be null if no training title is assigned
Should accurately reflect the name of the training program
data_quality_checks: Ensure that the title is descriptive and relevant to the training content
Check for consistency in naming conventions across different training titles
metadata.business_description: The training title is used to identify and categorize the specific training programs that employees participate in, facilitating tracking and reporting on training activities.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by training type to analyze specific categories of training
Filter by completion status to assess training effectiveness
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of training titles by training type
GROUP BY training title to analyze participation rates

columns.name: transcript_status
columns.description: Indicates the current status of a training transcript for an employee.

business_rules: Can be null if the training has not been assigned yet
Must reflect valid status values such as 'Completed', 'In Progress', 'Not Started'
data_quality_checks: Ensure that the status values are consistent and adhere to predefined categories
Check for null values where training has been assigned
metadata.business_description: This column represents the status of the training transcript, which can indicate whether the training has been completed, is in progress, or has not been started. It is essential for tracking employee training progress and compliance with training requirements.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by status to analyze training completion rates
Filter by user_id to view individual training progress
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of transcripts by status
Percentage of completed transcripts

columns.name: division
columns.description: A field representing the division within the organization to which an employee belongs.

business_rules: Can be null if the employee is not assigned to a specific division
Should reflect the current organizational structure
data_quality_checks: Ensure that the division names are consistent with the official organizational chart
Check for any spelling errors or discrepancies in division names
metadata.business_description: This column indicates the specific division of the company that is responsible for the training or learning activities associated with the employee. It helps in categorizing training data by organizational structure.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by division to analyze training effectiveness within specific areas
Filter by division to assess compliance with training requirements
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT by division to analyze training participation
SUM of training hours by division for resource allocation

columns.name: position
columns.description: The role or job title of an employee within the organization, indicating their responsibilities and level within the hierarchy.

business_rules: Can be null if the employee's position is not defined at the time of training assignment.
Should accurately reflect the employee's current position to ensure proper training alignment.
data_quality_checks: Ensure that the position is a valid entry from a predefined list of positions within the organization.
Check for consistency in naming conventions (e.g., avoid abbreviations unless standardized).
metadata.business_description: This column represents the position held by an employee, which is crucial for understanding their role in training and development activities.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by specific positions to analyze training effectiveness
Filter by department to assess training needs across roles
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of training sessions by position
AVERAGE completion rate by position

columns.name: assigned_date
columns.description: The date on which the training was assigned to the employee.

business_rules: Can be null if the training has not yet been assigned
Should be a valid date format (YYYY-MM-DD)
data_quality_checks: Ensure the date is in the correct format
Check for logical consistency with other date fields (e.g., due_date, completed_date)
metadata.business_description: This column represents the date when a specific training program was assigned to an employee, which is crucial for tracking training timelines and compliance with training requirements.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by assigned_date to view trainings assigned within a specific timeframe
Filter by training_type to analyze assignments by type of training
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of trainings assigned by date
GROUP BY assigned_date to analyze training trends over time

columns.name: completed_date
columns.description: The date on which the training was completed by the employee.

business_rules: Can be null if the training is not yet completed
Should be a valid date format (YYYY-MM-DD)
If provided, must be on or after the assigned_date
data_quality_checks: Check for valid date format
Ensure that completed_date is not earlier than assigned_date
Validate that completed_date is not in the future
metadata.business_description: This column represents the date when an employee has successfully completed a training program. It is crucial for tracking training progress and compliance with learning objectives.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by completed_date range
Filter by training_type or curriculum_title
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of trainings completed by date
Average time taken to complete training

columns.name: due_date
columns.description: The date by which a training or learning activity is expected to be completed.

business_rules: Can be null if the training is ongoing or not yet assigned
Must be a valid date format (YYYY-MM-DD)
Should not be in the past for newly assigned trainings
data_quality_checks: Ensure the date is in the correct format
Check for logical consistency (e.g., due date should not be before assigned date)
Validate against training schedules to ensure compliance
metadata.business_description: This column represents the deadline for completing a specific training or learning module. It is crucial for tracking compliance with training schedules and ensuring timely completion of required training.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by due date range
Filter by training type or division
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of trainings due by date
SUM of trainings completed by due date

columns.name: satisfied_late
columns.description: Indicates whether the training was completed late but the employee is still satisfied with the training experience.

business_rules: Can be null if the training has not been completed
If completed late, the value must reflect the employee's satisfaction
data_quality_checks: Check for consistency with completion dates
Validate that satisfaction can only be recorded if the training is marked as completed
metadata.business_description: This column captures the satisfaction level of employees who completed their training after the due date, providing insights into the effectiveness of training programs even when deadlines are missed.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by training type
Filter by division or position
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of satisfied late vs. not satisfied late
Percentage of satisfied late completions

columns.name: created_at
columns.description: Timestamp indicating when the training record was created.

business_rules: Can be null, indicating that the creation date is not always recorded.
If present, it must be a valid timestamp format.
data_quality_checks: Ensure that the timestamp is in the correct format (YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS).
Check for any null values and assess their impact on data analysis.
metadata.business_description: This column represents the date and time when a training record was initially created in the system. It is crucial for tracking the timeline of training activities and understanding the historical context of training data.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by specific date ranges to analyze training activity during certain periods.
Filter by training type to see when specific training programs were initiated.
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of records created within a specific time frame.
GROUP BY month or year to analyze training trends over time.

columns.name: updated_at
columns.description: Timestamp indicating when the training record was last updated.

business_rules: Can be null if the record has never been updated
Should reflect the most recent update to the training record
data_quality_checks: Ensure the timestamp is in the correct format
Check for logical consistency with other date fields (e.g., updated_at should be after created_at)
metadata.business_description: This column captures the last modification date and time of the training record, which is crucial for tracking changes and ensuring data accuracy in training management.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by updated_at to find recently modified records
Combine with other date fields for comprehensive analysis
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of records updated within a specific timeframe
Group by training_type to analyze update frequency

columns.description: id field

columns.description: full name field

columns.name: user_id
columns.description: user id field

metadata.data_lineage: Data is sourced from the CSOD (Cornerstone OnDemand) system, where training records are initially created and updated. This data is then integrated into the enterprise data ecosystem for analytics and reporting.
metadata.data_retention: Data is retained for a period of 5 years to comply with regulatory requirements and support historical analysis.
access_patterns: Frequent read access for reporting and analytics by HR and Learning & Development teams
Occasional write access for updates from the CSOD system and manual corrections
metadata.business_purpose: The Training Data table exists to centralize training records, enabling efficient tracking of employee training progress, compliance with training requirements, and analysis of training effectiveness. This supports the organization's goals of enhancing employee skills and ensuring compliance with industry standards.

enhanced_columns.description: Unique identifier for each training record in the Training Data table.
business_rules: Can be null, as not all training records may have an ID assigned.
Should be unique within the context of the training records.
data_quality_checks: Check for null values where applicable.
Ensure that IDs are unique across the training records.
enhanced_columns.business_description: This column represents the ID assigned to each training record, which helps in tracking and managing training sessions for employees.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by training type
Filter by assigned date
Filter by completion status
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of training records by training type
COUNT of training records by division

enhanced_columns.description: A field that contains the complete name of the individual, typically formatted as 'First Last'.
business_rules: Can be null if the individual prefers not to disclose their name
Should not contain special characters or numbers
data_quality_checks: Check for leading or trailing spaces
Validate that the name does not exceed 100 characters
Ensure that the name is not empty if provided
enhanced_columns.business_description: This column represents the full name of the employee or participant in the training program, which is essential for identifying individuals in training records.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by training type
Filter by division

enhanced_columns.description: A unique identifier for users in the training data system.
business_rules: Can be null, as not all users may have an associated training record.
Should be unique within the context of the training data table.
data_quality_checks: Check for null values to ensure proper linkage to user records.
Validate format to ensure it adheres to expected user ID patterns.
enhanced_columns.business_description: This column represents the user identification number associated with each individual participating in training programs. It is essential for tracking user-specific training records and linking them to other relevant data points.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by user ID to analyze training records for specific individuals.
Filter by training type to assess user participation in different training programs.
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of unique user IDs to determine the number of participants in training programs.

enhanced_columns.description: The name of the manager responsible for overseeing the training of employees.
business_rules: Can be null if no manager is assigned
Should reflect the current manager's name for accurate reporting
data_quality_checks: Ensure the name is formatted correctly (e.g., no special characters)
Validate against a list of active managers to avoid outdated entries
enhanced_columns.business_description: This column captures the name of the manager associated with the training records, providing context for managerial oversight and accountability in employee training and development.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by specific manager to analyze training effectiveness
Filter by training type to see manager involvement
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of training sessions by manager
GROUP BY manager_name for reporting purposes

enhanced_columns.description: Field indicating the type of training provided to employees, such as online, in-person, or hybrid.
business_rules: Can be null if the training type is not specified
Should align with predefined training categories established by the Learning & Talent Management team
data_quality_checks: Ensure values are consistent with the defined training types
Check for typos or variations in training type descriptions
enhanced_columns.business_description: This column categorizes the training sessions attended by employees, helping to identify the format and delivery method of the training.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by specific training types to analyze trends
Filter by completion status to assess effectiveness of training types
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT by training type
GROUP BY training type to analyze participation rates

enhanced_columns.description: Text field representing the title of the curriculum associated with training programs.
business_rules: Can be null, but should be populated when applicable
Should accurately reflect the curriculum being referenced
data_quality_checks: Ensure that the title is descriptive and relevant to the training content
Check for consistency in naming conventions across different curricula
enhanced_columns.business_description: The curriculum title is used to identify and categorize training programs within the Learning & Talent Management domain. It helps in organizing training records and provides clarity on the content being delivered to employees.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by curriculum title to view specific training programs
Filter by training type to analyze curricula related to specific training categories
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of training programs by curriculum title
GROUP BY curriculum title to analyze training distribution

enhanced_columns.description: Field representing the title of the training program or course attended by an employee.
business_rules: Can be null if no training title is assigned
Should accurately reflect the name of the training program
data_quality_checks: Ensure that the title is descriptive and relevant to the training content
Check for consistency in naming conventions across different training titles
enhanced_columns.business_description: The training title is used to identify and categorize the specific training programs that employees participate in, facilitating tracking and reporting on training activities.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by training type to analyze specific categories of training
Filter by completion status to assess training effectiveness
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of training titles by training type
GROUP BY training title to analyze participation rates

enhanced_columns.description: Indicates the current status of a training transcript for an employee.
business_rules: Can be null if the training has not been assigned yet
Must reflect valid status values such as 'Completed', 'In Progress', 'Not Started'
data_quality_checks: Ensure that the status values are consistent and adhere to predefined categories
Check for null values where training has been assigned
enhanced_columns.business_description: This column represents the status of the training transcript, which can indicate whether the training has been completed, is in progress, or has not been started. It is essential for tracking employee training progress and compliance with training requirements.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by status to analyze training completion rates
Filter by user_id to view individual training progress
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of transcripts by status
Percentage of completed transcripts

enhanced_columns.description: A field representing the division within the organization to which an employee belongs.
business_rules: Can be null if the employee is not assigned to a specific division
Should reflect the current organizational structure
data_quality_checks: Ensure that the division names are consistent with the official organizational chart
Check for any spelling errors or discrepancies in division names
enhanced_columns.business_description: This column indicates the specific division of the company that is responsible for the training or learning activities associated with the employee. It helps in categorizing training data by organizational structure.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by division to analyze training effectiveness within specific areas
Filter by division to assess compliance with training requirements
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT by division to analyze training participation
SUM of training hours by division for resource allocation

enhanced_columns.description: The role or job title of an employee within the organization, indicating their responsibilities and level within the hierarchy.
business_rules: Can be null if the employee's position is not defined at the time of training assignment.
Should accurately reflect the employee's current position to ensure proper training alignment.
data_quality_checks: Ensure that the position is a valid entry from a predefined list of positions within the organization.
Check for consistency in naming conventions (e.g., avoid abbreviations unless standardized).
enhanced_columns.business_description: This column represents the position held by an employee, which is crucial for understanding their role in training and development activities.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by specific positions to analyze training effectiveness
Filter by department to assess training needs across roles
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of training sessions by position
AVERAGE completion rate by position

enhanced_columns.description: The date on which the training was assigned to the employee.
business_rules: Can be null if the training has not yet been assigned
Should be a valid date format (YYYY-MM-DD)
data_quality_checks: Ensure the date is in the correct format
Check for logical consistency with other date fields (e.g., due_date, completed_date)
enhanced_columns.business_description: This column represents the date when a specific training program was assigned to an employee, which is crucial for tracking training timelines and compliance with training requirements.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by assigned_date to view trainings assigned within a specific timeframe
Filter by training_type to analyze assignments by type of training
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of trainings assigned by date
GROUP BY assigned_date to analyze training trends over time

enhanced_columns.description: The date on which the training was completed by the employee.
business_rules: Can be null if the training is not yet completed
Should be a valid date format (YYYY-MM-DD)
If provided, must be on or after the assigned_date
data_quality_checks: Check for valid date format
Ensure that completed_date is not earlier than assigned_date
Validate that completed_date is not in the future
enhanced_columns.business_description: This column represents the date when an employee has successfully completed a training program. It is crucial for tracking training progress and compliance with learning objectives.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by completed_date range
Filter by training_type or curriculum_title
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of trainings completed by date
Average time taken to complete training

enhanced_columns.description: The date by which a training or learning activity is expected to be completed.
business_rules: Can be null if the training is ongoing or not yet assigned
Must be a valid date format (YYYY-MM-DD)
Should not be in the past for newly assigned trainings
data_quality_checks: Ensure the date is in the correct format
Check for logical consistency (e.g., due date should not be before assigned date)
Validate against training schedules to ensure compliance
enhanced_columns.business_description: This column represents the deadline for completing a specific training or learning module. It is crucial for tracking compliance with training schedules and ensuring timely completion of required training.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by due date range
Filter by training type or division
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of trainings due by date
SUM of trainings completed by due date

enhanced_columns.description: Indicates whether the training was completed late but the employee is still satisfied with the training experience.
business_rules: Can be null if the training has not been completed
If completed late, the value must reflect the employee's satisfaction
data_quality_checks: Check for consistency with completion dates
Validate that satisfaction can only be recorded if the training is marked as completed
enhanced_columns.business_description: This column captures the satisfaction level of employees who completed their training after the due date, providing insights into the effectiveness of training programs even when deadlines are missed.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by training type
Filter by division or position
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of satisfied late vs. not satisfied late
Percentage of satisfied late completions

enhanced_columns.description: Timestamp indicating when the training record was created.
business_rules: Can be null, indicating that the creation date is not always recorded.
If present, it must be a valid timestamp format.
data_quality_checks: Ensure that the timestamp is in the correct format (YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS).
Check for any null values and assess their impact on data analysis.
enhanced_columns.business_description: This column represents the date and time when a training record was initially created in the system. It is crucial for tracking the timeline of training activities and understanding the historical context of training data.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by specific date ranges to analyze training activity during certain periods.
Filter by training type to see when specific training programs were initiated.
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of records created within a specific time frame.
GROUP BY month or year to analyze training trends over time.

enhanced_columns.description: Timestamp indicating when the training record was last updated.
business_rules: Can be null if the record has never been updated
Should reflect the most recent update to the training record
data_quality_checks: Ensure the timestamp is in the correct format
Check for logical consistency with other date fields (e.g., updated_at should be after created_at)
enhanced_columns.business_description: This column captures the last modification date and time of the training record, which is crucial for tracking changes and ensuring data accuracy in training management.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by updated_at to find recently modified records
Combine with other date fields for comprehensive analysis
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of records updated within a specific timeframe
Group by training_type to analyze update frequency

key_relationships: Links to employee records for comprehensive skill tracking
Associates with training programs and curricula for reporting and analysis
primary_use_cases: Tracking employee training assignments and completion statuses
Generating reports for compliance and managerial oversight
Analyzing training effectiveness and employee skill development

semantic_description.description: This table stores training data related to employees, capturing essential information about their training assignments, completions, and statuses within an organization.

key_columns.name: id
key_columns.description: Unique identifier for each training record
key_columns.business_significance: Primary key that ensures each training entry can be uniquely identified and referenced

key_columns.name: user_id
key_columns.description: Identifier for the employee undergoing training
key_columns.business_significance: Links training records to specific employees, facilitating tracking of individual training histories

key_columns.name: training_type
key_columns.description: Type of training being undertaken (e.g., mandatory, elective)
key_columns.business_significance: Helps categorize training for compliance and reporting purposes

key_columns.name: transcript_status
key_columns.description: Current status of the training transcript (e.g., completed, in progress)
key_columns.business_significance: Indicates the progress of training for each employee, essential for performance evaluations and compliance

key_columns.name: assigned_date
key_columns.description: Date when the training was assigned to the employee
key_columns.business_significance: Tracks when training responsibilities were allocated, aiding in deadline management

key_columns.name: completed_date
key_columns.description: Date when the training was completed by the employee
key_columns.business_significance: Essential for measuring training effectiveness and compliance with training requirements

semantic_description.table_purpose: To serve as a comprehensive record of employee training activities, including details on training types, curriculum, and completion statuses for effective training management and compliance tracking.
semantic_description.business_context: This table is integral to the human resources and training departments of an organization, enabling the management of employee training programs. It supports compliance with regulatory training requirements, tracks employee development, and informs performance management processes.

suggested_relationships.reasoning: Each training record is associated with a specific employee, allowing for comprehensive training history tracking per employee.
Each curriculum can encompass multiple training titles, facilitating structured training programs and content management.

performance_considerations: Indexing on key columns such as user_id and training_type to optimize query performance
Regular maintenance of the table to ensure efficient data retrieval and storage

recommendations: At least 2 tables are required for relationship analysis

tables.name: Finance Demo
tables.description: This table stores finance-related demo data, including transactional details, cost centers, and project information, enabling comprehensive financial analysis and reporting.

columns.name: id
columns.description: Unique identifier for records in the Finance Demo table, used to distinguish each entry.

business_rules: Can be null, indicating that some records may not have an ID assigned.
Should be unique within the context of the table to avoid confusion in data analysis.
data_quality_checks: Check for null values to ensure data completeness.
Validate that IDs are unique across the dataset.
metadata.business_description: This column represents a unique ID for each financial record, which can be used to track and reference specific transactions or entries within the finance dataset.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by specific ID to retrieve detailed information about a particular record.
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of records by ID to determine the number of entries.

columns.name: period_name
columns.description: A field representing the name of the period for which financial data is recorded.

business_rules: Can be null, but should be populated when available for meaningful analysis
Should represent a valid time period (e.g., 'Q1 2023', '2023-01', '2023-12')
data_quality_checks: Validate that the period name follows a consistent naming convention
Check for null values in reports where period data is critical
metadata.business_description: This column captures the specific time period associated with financial transactions, allowing for time-based analysis and reporting.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by specific period_name to analyze financial data for that time frame
Combine with other columns like country or project for more granular insights
aggregation_suggestions: GROUP BY period_name for time-based financial summaries
COUNT transactions per period_name for trend analysis

columns.name: country
columns.description: A field representing the country associated with financial transactions.

business_rules: Can be null, but should be populated when available for accurate reporting.
Should conform to standard country codes (e.g., ISO 3166-1 alpha-2).
data_quality_checks: Validate against a list of recognized country codes.
Check for null values in critical reports.
metadata.business_description: This column captures the country where the financial transaction occurred, which is essential for regional analysis and compliance reporting.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by specific countries for regional analysis.
Filter by country to assess compliance with local regulations.
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT by country
SUM transactional_net by country

columns.name: ibx
columns.description: Field representing the IBX code related to financial transactions.

business_rules: Can be null
Should follow the defined IBX code format if populated
data_quality_checks: Validate format against IBX code standards
Check for null values where applicable
metadata.business_description: The IBX field is used to categorize and identify specific financial transactions within the Finance Demo table, aiding in the analysis of training and talent management expenditures.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by specific IBX codes to analyze related transactions
Filter by period_name to assess financial trends over time
aggregation_suggestions: GROUP BY ibx for financial reporting
COUNT ibx occurrences for transaction analysis

columns.name: parent_cost_center
columns.description: Field representing the parent cost center associated with a financial transaction.

business_rules: Can be null if the cost center is a top-level center
Should reference a valid cost center in the organization
data_quality_checks: Ensure that any non-null values correspond to existing cost centers
Check for consistency in naming conventions across cost centers
metadata.business_description: This column indicates the higher-level cost center under which the current cost center falls, allowing for hierarchical financial reporting and analysis.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by specific parent cost center to analyze related transactions
Filter by period_name to view historical data for a specific cost center
aggregation_suggestions: Aggregate by parent cost center to analyze total expenditures
Count transactions per parent cost center for reporting

columns.name: project
columns.description: Field representing the project associated with training and talent management activities.

business_rules: Can be null, as not all records may be associated with a specific project.
Should be descriptive enough to identify the project clearly.
data_quality_checks: Ensure that the project name is not excessively long (e.g., limit to 255 characters).
Validate that the project name does not contain special characters that may affect reporting.
metadata.business_description: This column captures the specific project under which training, learning, and talent management activities are categorized, allowing for better tracking and reporting of related data.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by project to view specific training data related to that project.
Combine with other columns like 'country' or 'account' for more granular analysis.
aggregation_suggestions: GROUP BY project to analyze training data by project.
COUNT(project) to determine the number of training activities per project.

columns.name: account
columns.description: A field representing the account associated with financial transactions in the Finance Demo table.

business_rules: Can be null, but should be populated when applicable
Should conform to the expected account naming conventions
data_quality_checks: Check for valid account formats
Ensure consistency in account naming across records
metadata.business_description: This column captures the account information related to various financial activities, which is essential for tracking and reporting on financial performance within the Learning & Talent Management domain.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by account to analyze specific financial activities
Filter by period_name to view account activities over time
aggregation_suggestions: GROUP BY account for financial reporting
SUM transactional_net by account for total expenses

columns.name: journal_source
columns.description: Field indicating the source of the journal entry in the finance system.

business_rules: Can be null if the source is unknown
Should be descriptive enough to identify the source of the journal entry
data_quality_checks: Ensure that the source is a valid entry from a predefined list of sources
Check for consistency in naming conventions
metadata.business_description: This column captures the origin of the journal entries, which can help in tracking the source of financial transactions and understanding their context within the finance demo.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by specific journal sources
Filter by period_name to analyze trends over time
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT by journal source
SUM of transactional_net grouped by journal source

columns.name: journal_category
columns.description: A field representing the category of the journal entry in the finance demo table.

business_rules: Can be null, but should be populated when applicable to enhance reporting
Values should be consistent with predefined categories to ensure uniformity
data_quality_checks: Check for consistency with predefined journal categories
Validate against a list of acceptable journal categories to avoid typos or incorrect entries
metadata.business_description: This column categorizes journal entries to facilitate better organization and reporting of financial transactions related to training and talent management activities.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by specific journal categories to narrow down financial reports
Filter by period_name to analyze journal categories over specific time frames
aggregation_suggestions: GROUP BY journal_category to analyze financial data by category
COUNT journal_category to determine the frequency of each category

columns.name: fah_event_type
columns.description: Type of event recorded in the Finance Demo table related to training and learning activities.

business_rules: Can be null, but should be populated when applicable
Values should correspond to predefined event types relevant to training and learning
data_quality_checks: Ensure values match the predefined list of event types
Check for consistency in event type naming conventions
metadata.business_description: This column captures the specific type of event associated with financial transactions in the context of learning and talent management, such as training sessions, workshops, or certifications.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by specific event types
Filter by period_name to analyze events over time
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT by event type
SUM of transactional_net grouped by event type

columns.name: po_number
columns.description: A unique identifier for purchase orders used in financial transactions.

business_rules: Can be null, but should be populated when applicable
Should follow the organization's format for purchase order numbers
data_quality_checks: Validate format against organizational standards
Check for duplicates within the same period
metadata.business_description: The purchase order number is used to track and manage procurement activities within the finance department. It links financial transactions to specific orders, facilitating budget management and expense tracking.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by period_name
Filter by country
Filter by project
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of purchase orders
SUM of transactional_net grouped by po_number

columns.name: transactional_net
columns.description: Numeric field representing the net amount of transactions recorded in the finance system.

business_rules: Can be null, but should be populated for all completed transactions
Must be a numeric value
data_quality_checks: Ensure values are numeric and within expected ranges
Check for null values in completed transactions
metadata.business_description: This column captures the net value of financial transactions, which is essential for understanding the overall financial performance related to training and talent management activities.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by period_name
Filter by country
Filter by project
aggregation_suggestions: SUM
AVERAGE

columns.name: functional_net
columns.description: Numeric field representing the net value of functional activities within the finance domain.

business_rules: Can be null, indicating no financial impact for certain records
Should be a numeric value, representing monetary amounts
data_quality_checks: Ensure values are numeric and within expected ranges
Check for null values where applicable
metadata.business_description: This column captures the net financial impact of various functional activities related to training and talent management, allowing for analysis of costs and investments in employee development.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by period_name to analyze trends over time
Filter by country to assess regional financial impacts
aggregation_suggestions: SUM for total functional net across periods
AVERAGE for average functional net per project

columns.name: created_at
columns.description: Timestamp indicating when the record was created, including time zone information.

business_rules: Can be null, indicating that the creation time may not always be recorded.
Should be populated when a new record is created.
data_quality_checks: Ensure that the timestamp is in the correct format (ISO 8601).
Check for null values where creation time is expected.
metadata.business_description: This column represents the date and time when a financial record was created, which is crucial for tracking the timing of transactions and events in the finance demo context.
filtering_suggestions: Filter records by creation date to analyze data from specific periods.
Filter by time zone if applicable to understand regional data trends.
aggregation_suggestions: Aggregate by month to analyze trends in record creation over time.
Count records created within specific time frames.

columns.name: updated_at
columns.description: Timestamp indicating when the record was last updated, including time zone information.

business_rules: Can be null if the record has never been updated.
Should reflect the most recent update time for the record.
data_quality_checks: Ensure the timestamp is in the correct format (ISO 8601).
Check for consistency with the created_at timestamp (updated_at should not be earlier than created_at).
metadata.business_description: This column represents the last modification date and time of the record, which is crucial for tracking changes and ensuring data accuracy in financial reporting.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by specific time ranges to analyze updates over periods.
Filter by records that have been updated within a certain timeframe.

metadata.data_lineage: Data is sourced from the CSOD (Cornerstone OnDemand) platform, integrated into the enterprise data ecosystem through ETL processes that harmonize and centralize the information.
metadata.data_retention: Data is retained for a period of 5 years to comply with financial reporting and auditing standards.
access_patterns: Frequent read access for reporting and analysis by business intelligence teams
Occasional write access for updates from integrated systems
metadata.business_purpose: The Finance Demo table centralizes financial data related to training and talent management, providing stakeholders with insights into costs, budgeting, and financial performance associated with learning initiatives.

enhanced_columns.description: Unique identifier for records in the Finance Demo table, used to distinguish each entry.
business_rules: Can be null, indicating that some records may not have an ID assigned.
Should be unique within the context of the table to avoid confusion in data analysis.
data_quality_checks: Check for null values to ensure data completeness.
Validate that IDs are unique across the dataset.
enhanced_columns.business_description: This column represents a unique ID for each financial record, which can be used to track and reference specific transactions or entries within the finance dataset.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by specific ID to retrieve detailed information about a particular record.
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of records by ID to determine the number of entries.

enhanced_columns.description: A field representing the name of the period for which financial data is recorded.
business_rules: Can be null, but should be populated when available for meaningful analysis
Should represent a valid time period (e.g., 'Q1 2023', '2023-01', '2023-12')
data_quality_checks: Validate that the period name follows a consistent naming convention
Check for null values in reports where period data is critical

filtering_suggestions: Filter by specific period_name to analyze financial data for that time frame
Combine with other columns like country or project for more granular insights
aggregation_suggestions: GROUP BY period_name for time-based financial summaries
COUNT transactions per period_name for trend analysis

enhanced_columns.description: A field representing the country associated with financial transactions.
business_rules: Can be null, but should be populated when available for accurate reporting.
Should conform to standard country codes (e.g., ISO 3166-1 alpha-2).
data_quality_checks: Validate against a list of recognized country codes.

enhanced_columns.business_description: This column captures the country where the financial transaction occurred, which is essential for regional analysis and compliance reporting.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by specific countries for regional analysis.
Filter by country to assess compliance with local regulations.
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT by country
SUM transactional_net by country

enhanced_columns.description: Field representing the IBX code related to financial transactions.
business_rules: Can be null
Should follow the defined IBX code format if populated
data_quality_checks: Validate format against IBX code standards
Check for null values where applicable
enhanced_columns.business_description: The IBX field is used to categorize and identify specific financial transactions within the Finance Demo table, aiding in the analysis of training and talent management expenditures.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by specific IBX codes to analyze related transactions
Filter by period_name to assess financial trends over time
aggregation_suggestions: GROUP BY ibx for financial reporting

enhanced_columns.description: Field representing the parent cost center associated with a financial transaction.
business_rules: Can be null if the cost center is a top-level center
Should reference a valid cost center in the organization
data_quality_checks: Ensure that any non-null values correspond to existing cost centers
Check for consistency in naming conventions across cost centers
enhanced_columns.business_description: This column indicates the higher-level cost center under which the current cost center falls, allowing for hierarchical financial reporting and analysis.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by specific parent cost center to analyze related transactions
Filter by period_name to view historical data for a specific cost center
aggregation_suggestions: Aggregate by parent cost center to analyze total expenditures
Count transactions per parent cost center for reporting

enhanced_columns.description: Field representing the project associated with training and talent management activities.
business_rules: Can be null, as not all records may be associated with a specific project.
Should be descriptive enough to identify the project clearly.
data_quality_checks: Ensure that the project name is not excessively long (e.g., limit to 255 characters).

enhanced_columns.description: A field representing the account associated with financial transactions in the Finance Demo table.
business_rules: Can be null, but should be populated when applicable
Should conform to the expected account naming conventions
data_quality_checks: Check for valid account formats
Ensure consistency in account naming across records
enhanced_columns.business_description: This column captures the account information related to various financial activities, which is essential for tracking and reporting on financial performance within the Learning & Talent Management domain.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by account to analyze specific financial activities
Filter by period_name to view account activities over time
aggregation_suggestions: GROUP BY account for financial reporting
SUM transactional_net by account for total expenses

enhanced_columns.description: Field indicating the source of the journal entry in the finance system.
business_rules: Can be null if the source is unknown
Should be descriptive enough to identify the source of the journal entry
data_quality_checks: Ensure that the source is a valid entry from a predefined list of sources
Check for consistency in naming conventions
enhanced_columns.business_description: This column captures the origin of the journal entries, which can help in tracking the source of financial transactions and understanding their context within the finance demo.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by specific journal sources
Filter by period_name to analyze trends over time
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT by journal source
SUM of transactional_net grouped by journal source

enhanced_columns.description: A field representing the category of the journal entry in the finance demo table.
business_rules: Can be null, but should be populated when applicable to enhance reporting
Values should be consistent with predefined categories to ensure uniformity
data_quality_checks: Check for consistency with predefined journal categories
Validate against a list of acceptable journal categories to avoid typos or incorrect entries
enhanced_columns.business_description: This column categorizes journal entries to facilitate better organization and reporting of financial transactions related to training and talent management activities.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by specific journal categories to narrow down financial reports
Filter by period_name to analyze journal categories over specific time frames
aggregation_suggestions: GROUP BY journal_category to analyze financial data by category
COUNT journal_category to determine the frequency of each category

enhanced_columns.description: Type of event recorded in the Finance Demo table related to training and learning activities.
business_rules: Can be null, but should be populated when applicable
Values should correspond to predefined event types relevant to training and learning
data_quality_checks: Ensure values match the predefined list of event types
Check for consistency in event type naming conventions
enhanced_columns.business_description: This column captures the specific type of event associated with financial transactions in the context of learning and talent management, such as training sessions, workshops, or certifications.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by specific event types
Filter by period_name to analyze events over time
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT by event type
SUM of transactional_net grouped by event type

enhanced_columns.description: A unique identifier for purchase orders used in financial transactions.
business_rules: Can be null, but should be populated when applicable
Should follow the organization's format for purchase order numbers
data_quality_checks: Validate format against organizational standards
Check for duplicates within the same period
enhanced_columns.business_description: The purchase order number is used to track and manage procurement activities within the finance department. It links financial transactions to specific orders, facilitating budget management and expense tracking.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by period_name
Filter by country
Filter by project
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of purchase orders
SUM of transactional_net grouped by po_number

enhanced_columns.description: Numeric field representing the net amount of transactions recorded in the finance system.
business_rules: Can be null, but should be populated for all completed transactions
Must be a numeric value
data_quality_checks: Ensure values are numeric and within expected ranges
Check for null values in completed transactions
enhanced_columns.business_description: This column captures the net value of financial transactions, which is essential for understanding the overall financial performance related to training and talent management activities.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by period_name
Filter by country
Filter by project
aggregation_suggestions: SUM
AVERAGE

enhanced_columns.description: Numeric field representing the net value of functional activities within the finance domain.
business_rules: Can be null, indicating no financial impact for certain records
Should be a numeric value, representing monetary amounts
data_quality_checks: Ensure values are numeric and within expected ranges
Check for null values where applicable
enhanced_columns.business_description: This column captures the net financial impact of various functional activities related to training and talent management, allowing for analysis of costs and investments in employee development.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by period_name to analyze trends over time
Filter by country to assess regional financial impacts
aggregation_suggestions: SUM for total functional net across periods
AVERAGE for average functional net per project

enhanced_columns.description: Timestamp indicating when the record was created, including time zone information.
business_rules: Can be null, indicating that the creation time may not always be recorded.
Should be populated when a new record is created.
data_quality_checks: Ensure that the timestamp is in the correct format (ISO 8601).
Check for null values where creation time is expected.
enhanced_columns.business_description: This column represents the date and time when a financial record was created, which is crucial for tracking the timing of transactions and events in the finance demo context.
filtering_suggestions: Filter records by creation date to analyze data from specific periods.
Filter by time zone if applicable to understand regional data trends.
aggregation_suggestions: Aggregate by month to analyze trends in record creation over time.
Count records created within specific time frames.

enhanced_columns.description: Timestamp indicating when the record was last updated, including time zone information.
business_rules: Can be null if the record has never been updated.
Should reflect the most recent update time for the record.
data_quality_checks: Ensure the timestamp is in the correct format (ISO 8601).
Check for consistency with the created_at timestamp (updated_at should not be earlier than created_at).
enhanced_columns.business_description: This column represents the last modification date and time of the record, which is crucial for tracking changes and ensuring data accuracy in financial reporting.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by specific time ranges to analyze updates over periods.
Filter by records that have been updated within a certain timeframe.

key_relationships: Links to cost center data for budget tracking
Associates with project management data for financial accountability
primary_use_cases: Analyzing training costs by country and project
Tracking financial performance of learning and development initiatives
Generating reports for compliance and managerial oversight

semantic_description.description: This table captures financial data related to various projects and cost centers, facilitating analysis and reporting of financial performance across different periods and countries.

key_columns.name: id
key_columns.description: Unique identifier for each record in the table
key_columns.business_significance: Acts as the primary key to ensure each entry is distinct and can be referenced uniquely

key_columns.name: period_name
key_columns.description: Name of the financial period for which the data is recorded
key_columns.business_significance: Essential for time-based financial analysis and reporting, allowing comparisons across different periods

key_columns.name: country
key_columns.description: Country associated with the financial data
key_columns.business_significance: Enables geographical analysis of financial performance and compliance with local regulations

key_columns.name: transactional_net
key_columns.description: Net amount of the transaction recorded
key_columns.business_significance: Critical for understanding the financial impact of transactions on the overall budget and financial health

key_columns.name: functional_net
key_columns.description: Functional net amount, possibly adjusted for specific accounting practices
key_columns.business_significance: Provides insights into the financial performance after accounting adjustments, aiding in accurate financial reporting

key_columns.name: created_at
key_columns.description: Timestamp indicating when the record was created
key_columns.business_significance: Useful for tracking data entry and changes over time, supporting audit trails and historical analysis

key_columns.name: updated_at
key_columns.description: Timestamp indicating when the record was last updated
key_columns.business_significance: Helps in monitoring data integrity and understanding the recency of the information

semantic_description.table_purpose: To serve as a repository for finance-related demo data, enabling stakeholders to track and analyze financial transactions, costs, and revenues associated with specific projects and accounts.
semantic_description.business_context: This table is part of a financial management system that supports budgeting, forecasting, and financial reporting. It allows organizations to analyze financial data across different dimensions such as time, geography, and project, thereby enhancing decision-making and strategic planning.

suggested_relationships.reasoning: Each cost center can have multiple financial records associated with it, allowing for detailed cost tracking and analysis
Each project can generate multiple financial transactions, enabling comprehensive project financial management
Multiple records can be associated with a single country, facilitating regional financial analysis and reporting

performance_considerations: Indexing on key columns such as period_name and country to optimize query performance
Monitoring for data growth to ensure efficient storage and retrieval

recommendations: At least 2 tables are required for relationship analysis

functions.name: completion_month
functions.description: This calculated column extracts the year and month from the completed date of training, formatted as 'YYYY-MM', to facilitate trend visualization of training completions over time.
functions.function_sql: CASE WHEN completed_date IS NOT NULL THEN TO_CHAR(completed_date, 'YYYY-MM') ELSE 'N/A' END

functions.name: training_completion_duration
functions.description: Calculates the total number of days taken by a user to finish training after assignment, accounting for null values and ensuring accurate results.

functions.name: completed_after_due
functions.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the training was finished past its due date. This column evaluates if the completion date of the training is later than the due date, accounting for null values.
functions.function_sql: CASE WHEN completed_date IS NOT NULL AND due_date IS NOT NULL AND completed_date > due_date THEN TRUE ELSE FALSE END

functions.name: manager_team_compliance
functions.description: Indicates whether all of a manager's direct reports have completed their assigned trainings. Returns TRUE if all trainings are completed, otherwise FALSE.
functions.function_sql: NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Training_Data t2 WHERE t2.manager_name = Training_Data.manager_name AND t2.is_completed = FALSE)

functions.name: normalized_completion_status
functions.description: Categorizes the training status of employees as 'On Time', 'Late', 'Pending', or 'Overdue' based on completion and due dates.
functions.function_sql: CASE WHEN is_completed IS TRUE AND completed_date IS NOT NULL AND completed_date <= due_date THEN 'On Time' WHEN is_completed IS TRUE AND completed_date IS NOT NULL AND completed_date > due_date THEN 'Late' WHEN is_completed IS FALSE AND due_date < CURRENT_DATE THEN 'Overdue' ELSE 'Pending' END
